```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
mu <- sample(seq(-5,5,0.5),1); sigma <- sample(seq(0.5,4,0.5),1)
```

Question
========
Para $Z=(X-`r mu`)/`r sigma`$, sabendo que $E(X)=`r mu`$ e $\operatorname{DP}(X)=`r sigma`$, quais são $E(Z)$ e $\operatorname{Var}(Z)$?

Answerlist
----------
* $E(Z)=0$ e $\operatorname{Var}(Z)=1$.
* $E(Z)=`r mu`$ e $\operatorname{Var}(Z)=`r sigma`$.
* $E(Z)=1$ e $\operatorname{Var}(Z)=0$.
* $E(Z)=0$ e $\operatorname{Var}(Z)=`r sigma^2`$.
* $E(Z)=`r mu/sigma`$ e $\operatorname{Var}(Z)=1/`r sigma^2`$.

Solution
========
Pela linearidade, $E(Z)=[E(X)-\mu]/\sigma=0$. Além disso,
$\operatorname{Var}(Z)=\operatorname{Var}(X)/\sigma^2=1$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S9Q3_padronizacao_momentos
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
